description: Confounder: The model assumes any time t derived from a standard diffusion sampling strategy

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at time t = 1/(1+√5).' does NOT hold here. The model assumes any time t derived from a standard diffusion sampling strategy (like cosine schedule) will behave similarly under min-SNR weighting. However, cosine_sample_median() returns 1 - cos(π/4) ≈ 0.2929, which gives SNR ≈ ((1−t)/t)² ≈ 5.828 > 5, so the weight becomes 5/(5.828+1) ≈ 0.732, not 5/6.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at time t = 1/(1+√5).' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
